name: Process Technician
description: Job Description: Now hiring! Process Technician Chattanooga, TN At BASF, we create chemistry through the power of connected minds. By balancing economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility, we are building a more sustainable future through chemistry. As the world’s leading chemical company, we help our customers in nearly every industry meet the current and future needs of society through science and innovation. We provide a challenging and rewarding work environment with a strong emphasis on process safety, as well as the safety of our employees and the communities we operate in and are always working to form the best team—especially from within, through an emphasis on lifelong learning and development. In the past year, BASF has been recognized as a DiversityInc Top 50 Company for Diversity; achieved a 100% compliance ranking on the Human Rights Campaign Corporate Equality Index measures of best places to work for LGBTQ employees; and been named a Best Employer for Diversity by Forbes. Come join us on our journey to create solutions for a sustainable solution! Come create chemistry with us! During your 12 hour rotating shift as a Process Technician We are looking for a talented individual like yourself to be responsible for the daily operation of the BASF Chattanooga chemical plant facility. In this role, you will be responsible for: Make-up of solutions from batch sheets and formula cards, transfer of solutions/batches, loading/unloading chemicals to and from tanks, trucks, and railcars. Use and understanding of basic process controls such as control valves, flows, pressures, levels, and temperatures. Use and understanding of processing equipment such as compressors, pumps, reactors, heat exchangers, steam boilers, valves, forklifts, hoists, flare systems, and other associated manufacturing equipment. Work in all weather conditions to complete various assigned job tasks for different plant processes. Willing and available to work rotating 12-hour shifts, with 32-day rotation between day and night shift. The Chattanooga site is a 24/7 operation, 365 days of the year, including weekends & holidays. Willing and available for overtime to cross train in additional production areas and support other members of the team by providing coverage. Assist in plant troubleshooting, prepping equipment for service, and returning equipment to service. Use of PPE specific to job task including Full Faced Respirator, where applicable #dispersionsresins&additives Formula for Success 2-year associate degree/technical degree in Chemical Process Technology or equivalent OR High School diploma with 2 years of Chemical, Manufacturing OR transferrable military, firefighter or mechanical repair experience. Basic computer skills using software packages such as Microsoft Office, SAP, etc. Able to read, comprehend and follow operating procedures with minimal supervision needed. Basic understanding of chemical processing equipment and general chemistry principals. Occupational health and safety, ecology, and process safety focus. Accountability for continuous improvement and basic understanding of lean six sigma principals. Ability to lift and carry up to 50lbs, with most job tasks limited to 25lbs or less and standing/walking for at least 8 hours of a 12-hour shift. Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future. In addition to competitive compensation and benefits, BASF provides you with access to a wide range of elements to help you be your best. It’s what we call you@BASF . We are committed to providing benefits, programs, and opportunities that support our employees’ overall well-being, personal growth, and a safe, collaborative, and inclusive work environment. Just some of the many benefits we offer include: Flexible work arrangements whenever possible Highly competitive retirement savings plan with company match and investment options Well-being programs that include comprehensive mental health support for you and your household family members Family forming benefits (fertility, adoption and surrogacy reimbursement, maternity/parental leave, and more) Back-up child and elder care with discount programs for families of all ages and stages Mentoring and career development opportunities that allow you to share, learn, and thrive Matching gifts program that allows you to deepen the impact of your contributions to qualified charities. Employee crisis support for when the unexpected happens Access to our BASF wine cellar, employee discounts, and much more! About us As one of the largest chemical companies in North America we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! At BASF we empower our employees with the tools, guidance and opportunities they need to advance and succeed in work and life. Giving you the support you need to be your best and fulfill your personal ambitions is what helps us create chemistry. After all, our success is linked to yours. Whatever path you envision, BASF is a great place to build a rewarding, successful career. Belong to Something Bigger. #belongatBASF Privacy statement BASF takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. If you have experienced any of the above, please contact myhr@basf.com to report fraud. Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis. Job Description Footer:

name: Senior Quality Specialist
description: Job Description: Now hiring! Senior Quality Specialist Greenville, OH  - Onsite - Domestic Relocation Availalable - We are looking for a Senior Quality Specialist to join our team in Greenville, OH. Come create chemistry with us! At BASF, we create chemistry through the power of connected minds. By balancing economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility, we are building a more sustainable future through chemistry. As the world’s leading chemical company, we help our customers in nearly every industry meet the current and future needs of society through science and innovation. The Senior Quality Specialist plays a critical role in ensuring that products, processes, and services consistently meet both internal and external quality standards. This position is instrumental in maintaining compliance with global frameworks such as IATF 16949, ISO 9001, VDA 6.3, and internal O&M requirements. You will champion Six Sigma continuous improvement initiatives to eliminate process inefficiencies and waste as well as share expertise through coaching and training team members to build quality awareness and capability. Provides expertise in disciplined methodologies for defining, measuring, analyzing, improving and controlling the quality in every one of the company's products, processes and transactions--with the goal of eliminating defects, improving efficiency and reducing costs.  These improvements are strategically critical in that they allow the company to continue to provide the goods and services required by our customers while operating in the lowest possible cost structure. As a Senior Quality Specialist, you create chemistry by... Key Duties: Provide expertise in disciplined methodologies for defining, measuring, analyzing, improving and controlling the quality in the company's products, processes and transactions--with the goal of eliminating defects, improving efficiency and reducing costs.  (These improvements are strategically critical in that they allow the company to continue to provide the goods and services required by our customers while operating in the lowest possible cost structure).Key DutiesLeading root cause investigations for non-conformances and implementing effective corrective and preventive actions (CAPA). Optimizing quality systems and ensuring alignment with global standards. Driving continuous improvement initiatives using Six Sigma methodology to eliminate process waste. Coaching and training associates to build organizational quality capability. Core Competencies & Tools Bulk Material & Manufacturing Process Expertise Automotive Core Tools (APQP, PPAP, FMEA, MSA, SPC) Data Analysis & Statistics for quality performance monitoring SAP QM Module (with focus on Non-Conformance Management) MinTab proficiency Ability to present to leadership and problem solve Office Suite & Dashboarding (Power BI and similar tools) If you have the following experience... Solid data analytics and statistical background required - preferably in automotive industry IATF and MiniTab required Degree in Engineering, Analytics, Chemistry or related field Strong Project Management Skills Lean/Six Sigma/Kaizen training & implementation experience Good communication, presentation, coaching, teaching, and interpersonal skills Comfortable working with all levels of management & staff History of successfully implemented Six Sigma Projects, Lean Manufacturing, IATF internal and external audits, Core Tolls, CSR, etc. Minimum 3 years’ experience working as a Black Belt Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future. In addition to competitive compensation and benefits, BASF provides you with access to a wide range of elements to help you be your best. It’s what we call you@BASF . We are committed to providing benefits, programs, and opportunities that support our employees’ overall well-being, personal growth, and a safe, collaborative, and inclusive work environment. Just some of the many benefits we offer include: Flexible work arrangements whenever possible Highly competitive retirement savings plan with company match and investment options Well-being programs that include comprehensive mental health support for you and your household family members Family forming benefits (fertility, adoption and surrogacy reimbursement, maternity/parental leave, and more) Back-up child and elder care with discount programs for families of all ages and stages Mentoring and career development opportunities that allow you to share, learn, and thrive Matching gifts program that allows you to deepen the impact of your contributions to qualified charities. Employee crisis support for when the unexpected happens Access to our BASF wine cellar, employee discounts, and much more! name: Laboratory Technician I
description: Job Description: Now hiring! Laboratory Technician I Geismar, LA We are looking for a Laboratory Technician I to join our Geismar team. Come create chemistry with us! This position is responsible for quality assurance testing of in process samples, raw materials, intermediates, and finished goods within the scope of the Company Quality Policy. Using appropriate software to evaluate data based on internal and external specifications, support the continuous improvement of lab and plant processes through QA/QC activities and meet and/or exceed customer expectations. As a Laboratory Technician I, you will - Analyze raw materials, intermediate products, finished products and other chemical samples and evaluate suitability according to established specifications to ensure quality and availability of final product - Analyze samples, provide quality data and become an active member along with the Lab Supervisor and Production personnel to provide innovative ideas to improve lab and plant processes and demonstrate continuous improvement - Know, understand and use software tools such as Lotus Notes, LabWare, SAP, Microsoft Office Programs, etc. which enhance quality reporting - Ensure the suitability of laboratory equipment through proper operation, maintenance, calibration, verification and good quality control and assessment activities. - Generate quality data and significant statistical data to support plant personnel in their efforts to enhance process control, meet customer requirements and continuously improve our product quality characteristics. If you... are able to work as part of a team, understand chemical and instrument measurement processes, be self-motivated, and willing to develop technical knowledge and understanding of the chemical and measurement processes involved in their respective units, you will need a - Minimum 2-year Associates degree in Chemistry or 2-year technical degree in analytical testing in Chemistry or related field is preferred. 0 - 2 years’ experience working in a chemical manufacturing laboratory environment. - Basic understanding of general chemistry - Basic understanding of theory, operation, calibration, maintenance and troubleshooting of laboratory instrumentation such as gas chromatographs, viscometers, spectrophotometers, pH meters, titrators, etc . - Basic understanding of the techniques of quality assurance and quality control - Basic understanding of math and statistics - Basic understanding of ISO, RCMS, and cGMP quality system requirements - Basic computer skills using software packages such as Microsoft Office, Power BI, SAP, Lotus Notes, etc. Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future. In addition to competitive compensation and benefits, BASF provides you with access to a wide range of elements to help you be your best. It’s what we call you@BASF . We are committed to providing benefits, programs, and opportunities that support our employees’ overall well-being, personal growth, and a safe, collaborative, and inclusive work environment. Just some of the many benefits we offer include: Flexible work arrangements whenever possible Highly competitive retirement savings plan with company match and investment options Well-being programs that include comprehensive mental health support for you and your household family members Family forming benefits (fertility, adoption and surrogacy reimbursement, maternity/parental leave, and more) Back-up child and elder care with discount programs for families of all ages and stages Mentoring and career development opportunities that allow you to share, learn, and thrive Matching gifts program that allows you to deepen the impact of your contributions to qualified charities. name: Material Handler - Greenville - 3rd shift
description: Job Description: Now hiring! Material Handler Greenville, OH $23.50 per/hour We are looking for a Material Handler to join our Coatings team in Greenville, OH. Come create chemistry with us! BASF’s Coatings division is a global expert in the development, production and marketing of innovative and sustainable automotive OEM and refinish coatings as well as applied surface treatments for metal, plastic and glass substrates in a wide range of industries. This portfolio is supplemented by "Beyond Paint Solutions", which enable new applications with innovative surfaces. As a Material Handler working the 3rd shift, you will lead in keeping production moving in your daily tasks. We value safety first and foremost as our number one priority over all else. As a Material Handler, you will create Chemistry by.... Loading and off-loading packaged materials Staging materials to support production Operating powered industrial such as forklifts and yard tractors Maintaining assigned inspections equipment and housekeeping duties Advancing to be multi-functional and knowledgeable regarding department tasks for Material Handlers Being willing to work in and as a part of a continuously developing team -oriented atmosphere Assisting other team members as workflow dictates and demonstrating a steady work pace with changing priorities Advancing skills and knowledge in relation to department duties is also required Supporting Process Safety programs through participation in compliance audits, Pre-Start up Safety Reviews, PHAs (Revalidations; Clean-Sheets) and other hazard / risk assessment activities; utilize available Participating in process safety training, related incident investigations and completing assigned action items in a timely manner Accepting personal responsibility to act according to process safety regulations and company directives Demonstrating understanding of primary hazards in work area and the safeguards in place to manage them as this is a safety sensitive position If you have... High School Diploma or GED 1 year of forklift experience in a warehouse setting preferred Hazardous Materials Handling Certification CFR49 Material handling, forklift and yard tractor operation, and experience with handling chemicals preferred. Strong safety commitment, teamwork skills, and a self-directed work ethic Ability to lift to 55lbs Familiarity with material handling safety/hygiene issues Computer skills to accomplish training opportunities Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future. name: Maintenance Technician (Mechanic)
description: Job Description: Now hiring! Maintenance Technician (Mechanic) Sparks, GA We are looking for a Maintenance Technician to join our Agricultural Solutions team in Sparks, GA Come create chemistry with us! BASF’s Agricultural Solutions division connects innovation, customers, partners and agricultural experts and integrates sustainability criteria into all business decisions. We help farmers deliver the best possible outcomes, working to achieve the balance between economic, environmental, and social value creation for sustainable and efficient agriculture. BASF's Crop Protection Site in Sparks, Georgia is seeking a Maintenance Technician who has mechanical aptitude, training, and the skills necessary to perform maintenance function on various plant infrastructures. The candidate will report to and receive work assignments, priorities, etc. from the Maintenance Supervisor. During your shift as a Maintenance Technician (Mechanic), you will Perform preventive and/or predictive maintenance of mechanical tasks, document findings and completion. Maintain, check, adjust, install, and repair every mechanical system in the plant, according to maintenance procedures and manufacturers recommendations using drawing, manuals, blueprints, and P&ID diagrams. Repair and install various piping systems and valves: including threaded, flanged, welded, glued, and compression Recommend changes to existing equipment, piping, or system in order to improve plant safety or reliability. Recommend tools, equipment, or methods to improve the safety or timeliness of system repairs. Support and endorse all company or regulatory standards regarding ecology, health, and safety at the site. Complete Root Cause Failure Analysis investigations If you... High School Diploma or GED Minimum Four years’ work experience in an industrial / manufacturing setting AND/OR two years’ work experience with a two-year industrial maintenance-related degree (technical, associates or certificates) Eight years’ work experience in an industrial / manufacturing setting (preferred) Knowledge of operation, maintenance, repair, and calibration of chemical process instrumentation and controls (Level, Flow, Pressure, and Temperature). Experience in Microsoft Applications such as Word, Excel, Internet Explorer, and Outlook Ability to write and revise job instructions. name: Customer Site Service Manager
description: Job Description: Now hiring! Customer Site Service Manager (CSSM) Southfield, MI BASF Coatings LLC is seeking a Customer Site Service Manager (CSSM) (Southfield, MI) to support the daily operation of BASF coatings products in customer automotive assembly plants by providing on-site technical and application oversight. Respond to customer requirements, enforce product quality/performance specification, and support new product/application development. Oversee product performance including color management, defect identification, process root causing and corrective actions implementation. Develop and support key customer relationships. Support the efforts of a BASF sales/technical team to assure value to the customer for product technical support, problem investigation, analysis, and resolution. Assure product performance meets or exceeds customer expectations and supports business growth objectives to be our customer’s supplier of choice. Control key performance indicators within product service environments to drive problem resolution and product improvement initiatives critical to customer performance expectations. Use ability to train and mentor members of the team to ensure the customer is receiving the best possible service. 15% domestic travel required. Telecommuting permitted. Supervise 6 direct reports holding the job titles of Field Technical Service Representatives. Job Requirements: The position requires a Bachelor’s degree in Chemical Engineering, Industrial Engineering, or related, and five (5) years of experience in OEM Paint Application. 15% domestic travel. Telecommuting permitted. Experience must include the following: All phases of the automotive paint application process, including quality, customer focus, and manufacturing Providing on-site expertise in coatings products, their application, performance, and problem resolution as needed Implementing action plans; tracking and updating customer metrics; and managing future plant production and new product introductions Inventory, forecasting, billing, and ordering management for a CPU (cost per unit) business model QUALIFIED APPLICANTS: Apply at https://basf.jobs , Requisition Code 135586.
